sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: June 2026 marked significant activity in targeted protein degradation (TPD) alongside broader pipeline shifts across the biopharmaceutical sector. Roche advanced its strategic partnership with Nurix Therapeutics for the BTK degrader bexobrutideg, signaling industry confidence in eliminating targets over traditional inhibition with a $700 million upfront commitment. Simultaneously, Amphista Therapeutics secured FDA clearance for its BRD9 degrader AMX-883, targeting acute myeloid leukaemia with a non-cereblon mechanism utilizing Eclipsys platform technology. These TPD advancements contrast with mixed clinical readouts elsewhere, such as Sanofi halting its riliprubart trial in chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy due to efficacy concerns. While complement inhibitors and cell therapies remain active in autoimmune indications, the strategic focus on protein degradation technologies by major players like Roche and emerging biotechs like Amphista highlights a shift toward novel mechanisms to overcome resistance in oncology. Investors are closely monitoring these degradation platforms as potential best-in-class solutions, even as traditional antibody programs face challenges. The convergence of big pharma capital and innovative biotech platforms suggests a robust pipeline for next-generation therapeutics capable of targeting previously undruggable proteins, though some source datasets noted a lull in specific PROTAC announcements relative to broader health policy updates during this period.

points.text: Roche — Bexobrutideg — Blood Cancers/Autoimmune — Collaboration with Nurix for BTK targeted protein degradation.
Amphista Therapeutics — AMX-883 — Acute Myeloid Leukaemia — FDA IND clearance for BRD9 degrader.
Nurix Therapeutics — Bexobrutideg — Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia — Up to $2.3 billion deal potential with Roche.
Roche: Strategic partnership with Nurix to co-develop BTK degrader bexobrutideg to overcome drug resistance.
Amphista Therapeutics: Advancing BRD9 degrader AMX-883 into clinical trials using Targeted Glue™ technology.
Nurix Therapeutics: Licensing deal for bexobrutideg with Roche covering US rights split, upfront payment, and royalties.
Bexobrutideg: BTK degrader, Phase 3 study in CLL expected to begin summer 2026, aims to sidestep resistance.
AMX-883: BRD9 degrader (DCAF16-dependent), Phase 1 monotherapy dose-escalation in relapsed/refractory AML and high-risk MDS.
Mechanism: Targeted protein degradation via Eclipsys platform distinct from cereblon or VHL-based PROTACs.
Roche and Nurix: $700 million upfront, up to $2.3 billion total, split US rights, Roche covers 60% development costs.
Amphista Therapeutics: IND clearance enables Phase 1 trial initiation in H2 2026 for AMX-883.
